root_text.an10.170:1.3: katamañca, bhikkhave, orimaṁ tīraṁ, katamañca pārimaṁ tīraṁ?
root_text.an10.170:1.4: Pāṇātipāto, bhikkhave, orimaṁ tīraṁ, pāṇātipātā veramaṇī pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.5: Adinnādānaṁ orimaṁ tīraṁ, adinnādānā veramaṇī pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.6: Kāmesumicchācāro orimaṁ tīraṁ, kāmesumicchācārā veramaṇī pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.7: Musāvādo orimaṁ tīraṁ, musāvādā veramaṇī pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.8: Pisuṇā vācā orimaṁ tīraṁ, pisuṇāya vācāya veramaṇī pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.9: Pharusā vācā orimaṁ tīraṁ, pharusāya vācāya veramaṇī pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.10: Samphappalāpo orimaṁ tīraṁ, samphappalāpā veramaṇī pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.11: Abhijjhā orimaṁ tīraṁ, anabhijjhā pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.12: Byāpādo orimaṁ tīraṁ, abyāpādo pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.13: Micchādiṭṭhi orimaṁ tīraṁ, sammādiṭṭhi pārimaṁ tīraṁ.
root_text.an10.170:1.14: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, orimaṁ tīraṁ, idaṁ pārimaṁ tīranti.

translation_text.an10.170:1.1: “Mendicants, I will teach you the near shore and the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak. …
translation_text.an10.170:1.3: And what, mendicants, is the near shore? What is the far shore?
translation_text.an10.170:1.4: Killing living creatures is the near shore, mendicants, and not killing living creatures is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.5: Stealing is the near shore, and not stealing is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.6: Sexual misconduct is the near shore, and avoiding sexual misconduct is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.7: Lying is the near shore, and not lying is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.8: Divisive speech is the near shore, and avoiding divisive speech is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.9: Harsh speech is the near shore, and avoiding harsh speech is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.10: Talking nonsense is the near shore, and avoiding talking nonsense is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.11: Covetousness is the near shore, and contentment is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.12: Ill will is the near shore, and good will is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.13: Wrong view is the near shore, and right view is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:1.14: This is the near shore, and this is the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:2.2: who cross to the far shore.
translation_text.an10.170:3.1: When the teaching is well explained,
translation_text.an10.170:3.3: are the ones who will cross over
translation_text.an10.170:3.4: Death’s dominion so hard to pass.
translation_text.an10.170:4.2: an astute person should develop the bright.
translation_text.an10.170:4.4: for the seclusion so hard to enjoy,
translation_text.an10.170:5.4: should cleanse themselves of mental corruptions.
translation_text.an10.170:6.1: And those whose minds are rightly developed
translation_text.an10.170:6.6: they are quenched in this world.”
